metadata.title: Fig. 3. Key milestones and policy evolution of air pollution control in China, including control actions on the emissions of  SO_2  and  NO_x  and on ambient concentrations of  PM_{2.5} . FYP: Five-Year Plan.
references: $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution (Fig. 3). Control actions on acid rain were primarily focused on  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions from coal combustion in the 1990s. However, the increasing trend of  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions was not reversed until 2007, when diversified technological and policy instruments were widely utilized. Such progress could not have been achieved without innovative improvement in political accountability, verification of  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions, and financial incentives for power plants to install and operate flue gas desulfurization (FGD).  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  has more sources than  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ , including coal burning, mobile sources, and other fossil fuel combustion sectors. Therefore, control actions on  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  emissions must be more extensive and complex, which requires cooperation among multiple sectors. Controlling  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations not only requires consistently mitigating multiple precursors from numerous sectors, but also demands consideration of complex influences from both meteorological and atmospheric chemistry conditions. Indeed, comprehensive control actions with multi-party coordination on provincial and even national levels have been implemented in China to minimize the adverse ecological and social impacts of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution. Since 2013, combating  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution has marked a strategic transfer from emission control toward air quality management in China, which is oriented toward benefiting the public health and social welfare. The fundamental improvement of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations has become a key indicator of the success of ecological civilization construction in China.

metadata.source_md: Progress of Air Pollution Control in China and Its.md
metadata.save_path: C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\Progress of Air Pollution Control in China and Its\figure_6.jpg
metadata.title: Fig. 6. (a) Trends in observed annual  PM_{2.5}  concentrations for BTH and 74 key cities between 2013 and 2017; (b) trends expressed as 90th percentiles in maximum daily average  8hO_3  concentration. Shaded areas represent the 25th-75th percentile range. Data are available in Table S1 in Appendix A. Details on monthly  PM_{2.5}  concentration for 74 key cities are summarized in Tables S2-S6.  PM_{2.5}  and  O_3  concentrations in the BTH region and 74 cities were obtained from the China National Environmental Monitoring Center.
references: Despite the substantial progress that has been made in addressing acid rain and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  issues (Fig. 6(a)), an unwanted increase in ground-level  $\mathrm{O}_3$  pollution is still found in many regions across China, posing the more complex challenge of simultaneous continuous mitigation of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution with an emerging  $\mathrm{O}_3$  issue. As  $\mathrm{O}_3$  pollution has been steadily increasing in most parts of eastern China [47-50],  $\mathrm{O}_3$  issues have recently gained more attention among both the public and academia in China. As illustrated in Fig. 6(b), the average 90th percentiles in daily maximum  $8\mathrm{h}$ $\mathrm{O}_3$  concentrations increased by  $20.1\%$  and  $24.5\%$  in 74 key cities and BTH, respectively, from 2013 to 2017. Furthermore, densely populated regions of eastern China were exposed to high  $\mathrm{O}_3$  pollution, increasing the impacts of  $\mathrm{O}_3$  on human health. The results of the GBD project [51] estimated that respiratory mortality attributable to long-term  $\mathrm{O}_3$  exposure in adults is between 274000 and 316000 in China, equal to approximately  $25\% -29\%$  of the deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure [16]. It is necessary to integrate  $\mathrm{O}_3$  and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  controls to ensure a pollution-free and healthy living environment in the development of ecological civilization in China.

The biggest challenge of  $\mathrm{O_3}$  pollution control is rooted in the complexity of atmospheric photochemistry formation, which depends on  $\mathrm{O_3 - NO_x - VOC}$  sensitivity diagnosis and meteorological conditions [52,53]. A control strategy designed to reduce emissions of  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  or VOCs for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  control may result in an unexpected disadvantage for  $\mathrm{O_3}$  reduction. Strong VOC-limiting systems are found in most urban areas of China [54,55], and  $\mathrm{O_3}$  in urban areas can be enhanced if few or no VOCs are controlled simultaneously with  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  [56]. For example, during the Action Plan from 2013 to 2017, anthropogenic emissions of  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  were reduced by  $21\%$  but VOCs increased by  $2\%$  nationwide [57]. Such combinational changes in  $\mathrm{NO}_x$ -VOC emissions might be beneficial for reducing  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  but not for  $\mathrm{O_3}$ , as shown by the decreased  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations and increased  $\mathrm{O_3}$  concentrations during the same period (Fig. 6). It is notable that the increase in  $\mathrm{O_3}$  concentration during the Action Plan period is also attributable to variation in meteorological conditions—specifically, changing temperature and short-wave radiation. Therefore, full consideration of meteorology and optimal mitigation of precursor emissions are critical in designing an effective  $\mathrm{O_3}$  control strategy.

metadata.save_path: C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\How the Air Clean Plan and carbon mitigation measu\figure_5.jpg
metadata.title: Fig. 5. Spatial distribution of changes in  PM_{2.5} -attributable premature death in mainland China (a), BTH (b), YRD (c), FWP (d), PRD region (e) between 2014 and 2020 (where the numbers of premature deaths in 2014 and 2020 are both zero this is represented by a white background).
references: The  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths was reduced in  $68.38\%$  areas of mainland China over 2014 to 2020 (Fig. 5). The most effectively controlled region was BTH, where over  $74\%$  of the area showed a decline in the number premature deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure. The FWP produced the smallest decrease amongst the four priority control regions, with the number of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths reducing in only  $26.95\%$  of the area. Regarding the decrease number of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths, unlike the largest decrease in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentration occurred in BTH region, the largest decrease in the number of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature death occurred in YRD region, with a decrease number of 0.037 million  $(17.00\%)$  in 2020 compared to 2014 (Table 1), followed by the PRD (decrease  $16.45\%$ ), BTH (decrease  $10.51\%$ ) and FWP (decrease  $8.99\%$ ) regions.

27010  
Fig. 4. Spatial distribution (at  $1\mathrm{km}$  resolution) of the  $\mathbf{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature death in mainland China (a), BTH (b), YRD (c), FWP (d), and PRD region (e) in 2020.

We provide a comprehensive estimation of the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations and the number of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths, as well as spatiotemporal changes in these, and a quantitative assessment of the effectiveness of ACP methods in mainland China during 2014 to 2020. The two key contributions are: (1) We estimated the spatiotemporal variation in the number of deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure in mainland China during ACP based on the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  product with the finest spatial resolution available to date, and elucidated the spatial discrepancies in policy implementation across China. China's recent clean air actions have achieved great benefits for human health and the environment. However, over  $98\%$  of the population still lived under the risk of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution ( $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations over  $15\mu \mathrm{g}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ ), with 1.12 million ( $95\%$  CI: 0.79-1.56) premature deaths caused by  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution in mainland China in 2020, accounting for  $\sim 27.05\%$  of global ambient  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths. In addition, the environmental and health benefits of China ACP are not fully synergistic in spatial pattern, with the greatest health benefits detected in the YRD region and the best environment benefits identified in the BTH region. To achieve the co-benefits of environment and health, specific policies need to be adopted under different conditions. (2) We quantified the environment and health co-benefits of ACP measures and carbon mitigation from 2014 to 2020. Clean production was the most effective abatement measure in China ACP, reducing net  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations and net premature deaths by  $18.35\mu \mathrm{g}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$  and 0.45 million, respectively. The co-effects of  $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$  and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  contributed to a  $23\%$  and  $26\%$  decrease in the number of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths and became one of the major drivers for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths reduction in the APWBSD phase. This confirms the clear environment and health co-benefits of carbon mitigation measures. Meanwhile, the effectiveness of process and source control measures reduced during 2018 to 2020. With the exhaustion of end-of-pipe and energy consumption control measures, the future air clean actions should focus more on carbon abatement and energy structure optimization, especially under the background of global climate change mitigation. In addition to mitigation in industrial and energy sectors, it is also critical to explore more cost-effective approaches (e.g., agricultural ammonia mitigation) (Zhou et al., 2021; Gu et al., 2021) for pollution control in other sectors.

metadata.title: Fig. 1. Timeline of CCTPs implemented in China during 2015-2020. (Note: the public information is collected from the National Energy Administration, and the illustration is made by the authors).
references: On the policy side, the Chinese government explicitly proposed to promote the clean and efficient use of coal in 2014. And subsequently, two significant specialized action plans were published in 2015 (Action Plan for Clean and Efficient Use of Coal in Industry and Action Plan for Clean and Efficient Use of Coal), signifying that the clean and efficient use of coal in China has entered the implementation stage since 2015 (Zhu, 2019). Herein, the former plan chose four coal-dominated industries as key objects, and defined 21 types of critical technologies for clean and efficient coal utilization in the industry; the latter plan proposed the subjects involved in CCTPs include the government, enterprises, market, and the public, and all of the coal-rich districts in China were selected as key regions. Currently, a series of laws, regulations, and policies have been brought into effect, to alleviate air pollution and accelerate the development of CCTs. Additionally, China's 13th Five-Year Plan (FYP) ranges from 2016 to 2020, which sets goals and directions for national economic and social development during this period. In response to the economic regulation and marker supervision, 2020 is set as the last implementation year of many CCTPs, as shown in Fig. 1. Overall, these policies directed the dominant logic of atmospheric administration in the 13th FYP, namely utilizing coal resources cleanly and efficiently, reducing emissions comprehensively, and decreasing coal consumption.
key_entities: Action Plan for Clean and Efficient Use of Coal

A tunnel in the city center of Shanghai  $(31.24^{\circ}\mathrm{N}, 121.50^{\circ}\mathrm{E})$  was selected to conduct air pollution monitoring. The tunnel is called East Yan'an Road tunnel, is an underwater tunnel crossing the Huangpu River in Shanghai. It has two bores with two lanes for each direction. The total length is  $2,107\mathrm{m}$ ; cross-sectional area is  $33.75\mathrm{m}^2$ . To continuously monitor major air pollutants from vehicle exhaust, we installed two integrated air pollution monitoring units at  $150\mathrm{m}$  away from the inlet and outlet of the tunnel, respectively. The height of the unit was about  $1.2\mathrm{m}$ . Fig. 2 shows the location of the tunnel and the monitoring sites. There were 4 sensors which could separately monitor NO,  $\mathrm{NO}_2$ , CO, and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations in each unit. NO,  $\mathrm{NO}_2$ , and CO were detected using electrochemical method. Their detection limits were  $0.01\mathrm{mg}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ ,  $0.01\mathrm{mg}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ , and  $0.15\mathrm{mg}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ , respectively.  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  was detected by light scattering method. Its resolution was  $0.1\mu \mathrm{g}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ . The monitoring data was continuously transmitted to a remote database through wireless network. The system was built in July 2016 and has been operated by now. To ensure the reliability of monitoring

metadata.title: Fig. 6. The variation trend of AQI in the Chinese mainland.
references: considerably decreased from 2013 to 2020. As shown in Fig. 6, the AQI of all studied cities was all less than 100 since 2017, which is the threshold AQI value of excessive pollutants, indicating that air quality in China began to pick up. Herein, for better comparison, the 31 provincial capital cities were categorized into 7 districts according to the official administrative geographical area division. An identical trend was seen to that in central China, which showed the greatest improvement in air quality during 7 years.

Clean and efficient use of coal in China entered the implementation stage in 2015, and a series of policies were established to promote the sustainable development of clean coal technologies (CCTs). However, the long-term effects of the clean coal technology policies (CCTPs) have not been evaluated, and the outbreak of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) would bring great uncertainty regarding the effectiveness evaluation. Employing deep learning approaches and spatial autocorrelation analysis, the present work intended to explore the air quality variation before and after the implementation of CCTPs, and investigate the association between air quality and socioeconomic factors to explore the internal mechanism of air quality improvement. Results showed that after implementing CCTPs, the air quality index (AQI) had an average reduction of  $18.82\%$  and the identical drop in air pollution in 2018 implied a 2-year time lag of CCTPs. Additionally, emission reduction and deindustrialization were explored as two promising ways to improve air quality while promoting energy transformation. The findings indicated that the Chinese government ought to pay more attention to long-term plans; industrial restructuring and environmental protection should be insisted upon to realize sustainable energy development. By providing a perspective on air quality improvement after the policies are implemented, this study can lead practitioners and academia to a comprehensive and objective view of clean coal policies.

The study obtained the data including a total of 1440 daily air quality and meteorological data (January 22, 2016, to December 31, 2019) from 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland,  $60\%$  of which are chosen as the training set,  $20\%$  as the validation set and  $20\%$  as the test set. After determining the best basic network architecture for the current prediction tasks, models are set up in units of cities, and the training set is used to train the tailored CNN-LSTM models. The accuracy indicators of the model prediction results of 31 cities, namely RMSE values averaging 22.06, models perform well overall, indicating that the applied CNN-LSTM network has good capabilities for AQI prediction (Jin et al., 2020; Mao et al., 2021).
Temporally, it is evident that the AQI in the Chinese mainland
Fig. 5. AQI regional spatial autocorrelation in 31 provincial cities of the Chinese mainland.
Fig. 7. The variation trend of AQI in four seasons.

During the period of CCTPs implementation, an obvious air quality improvement was observed in the Chinese mainland, manifested as a decrease of AQI value by  $18.82\%$  from 2013 to 2020. It is concluded that the clean and efficient transformation of coal resources conforms to China's national conditions, and could indeed improve the atmospheric environment, which has been demonstrated in the existing research as well (Li and Patino-Echeverri, 2017; Liu et al., 2021). Specifically, correlation coefficients showed that the promotion of emission reduction and deindustrialization could fundamentally help to reduce airborne contamination, although their persistence relies on continued efforts and is influenced by various factors (Yang and Teng, 2018). Another important observation was a 2-year time lag before the stringent CCTPs starting from 2015 takes any strong positive effects, shown as a dramatical reduction of AQI in 2018. It implies that the influence of time lag should be considered in the decision-making process, as a time lag commonly appears in air pollution regulations and plans (Van der et al., 2017).

The increase in population size and economic activity has remarkably increased the demand for fossil fuels in China, posing a severe threat to the environment. However, the recently implemented coal-based energy transition has shifted the energy structure from polluting energies to environment-friendly energies, and the subsequent policies created a great revolution in energy development and environment protection. Nevertheless, the energy distribution and air pollution in China are characterized by regionality, thus, targeting the efficiency of these national control policies, scholars mainly focus on regions with high-polluting and energy-intensive industries, such as Shanxi province and the BJH region; or target a specific policy, such as CTG and CTE policy. Differently, this study revealed the air quality improvement during the implementation of CCTPs at the national level. Clarifying the internal mechanism of these policies is significant when revising and implementing the following policies to achieve sustainable development. The quantitative assessment provided significant results: i) After implementing CCTPs, the Chinese mainland experienced air quality improvement, with the reduction of AQI averaging  $18.82\%$ ; ii) An identical reduction of air pollution was observed in 2018, indicating a 2-year time lag of the related control policies; iii) There were positive associations between emission reduction promoting, deindustrialization and air quality improvement, suggesting two promising ways to realize sustainable energy development. Through assessing the air quality changes before and after the implementation of CCTPs, it is expected to provide a scientific basis for policymakers and stakeholders to forge consensus on the establishment and enforcement of clean coal policies.

Their detection limits were  $0.01\mathrm{mg}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ ,  $0.01\mathrm{mg}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ , and  $0.15\mathrm{mg}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ , respectively.  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  was detected by light scattering method. The system was built in July 2016 and has been operated by now. To ensure the reliability of monitoring

Vehicle flow and average speed in the tunnel were detected by a buried induction coil and transmitted to a data platform in real time. The fleet compositions were recognized by automatic acquisition of vehicle license plate information through a high-definition video in the tunnel. Fig. 4 shows the average traffic flow and fleet composition in each hour of weekday and weekend. The data between August and November of 2016 were used in this study. Daily average vehicle flow and speed were  $34,818 \pm 2,214 \mathrm{veh} \cdot \mathrm{h}^{-1}$  and  $33.47 \pm 7.95 \mathrm{km} \cdot \mathrm{h}^{-1}$  on weekday and were  $30,818 \pm 1,848 \mathrm{veh} \cdot \mathrm{h}^{-1}$  and  $33.86 \pm 8.83 \mathrm{km} \cdot \mathrm{h}^{-1}$  on weekend. Vehicle flows in peak hour were  $2,173 \pm 161$  and  $1,952 \pm 68 \mathrm{veh} \cdot \mathrm{h}^{-1}$  on weekday and weekend, respectively. East Yan'an Road tunnel was located in the city center of Shanghai, where heavy-duty diesel vehicles (HDDVs) were restricted to access except for some medium-duty diesel vehicles (MDDVs). Therefore, fleet in the tunnel was mainly comprised of light-duty gasoline vehicles, which accounted for  $93.7 \pm 2.2\%$  and  $94.6 \pm 1.9\%$  of the total on weekday and weekend, respectively. The

The CO,  $\mathrm{NO}_x$ , and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  EFs were  $0.91 \pm 0.47 - 1.47 \pm 0.63$ ,  $0.15 \pm 0.07 - 0.33 \pm 0.17\mathrm{gkm}^{-1} \cdot \mathrm{veh}^{-1}$ , and  $2 \pm 2 - 4 \pm 3\mathrm{mgkm}^{-1} \cdot \mathrm{veh}^{-1}$ , respectively. Their results were the lowest compared with other tunnel studies in China mainly because Taiwan implemented Tier 1 and Tier 2 emission standards in 1999 and 2008, earlier than the mainland of China. Liu et al. (2015a,b) and Deng et al. (2015) measured CO and  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  EFs in East Yan'an Road tunnel in 2012. CO EF decreased by about  $30\%$  compared with our result in 2016.  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  EF also declined if we considered  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  was similar to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  for vehicle exhaust. The enforcement of vehicle emission control strategies should be the main reason for the decrease. Between 2012 and 2016, Shanghai implemented the emission standards of LDGVs from Euro 4 to Euro 5, eliminated Euro 0 gasoline vehicles from the whole city, and restricted Euro 1 gasoline vehicles in urban area. CO and  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  EFs in Yingpan Road tunnel were  $0.75 \pm 0.56 - 6.05 \pm 5.94$  and  $0.12 \pm 0.02 - 0.82 \pm 0.76\mathrm{gkm}^{-1} \cdot \mathrm{veh}^{-1}$  (Deng et al., 2015). Our results were relatively lower than the average value of Yingpan Road tunnel. The fractions of gasoline and diesel vehicles have influences on EFs in the tunnels. CO,  $\mathrm{NO}_x$ , and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  EFs in Zhujiang tunnel were much higher than those in our study since there was higher proportion of diesel vehicles (Liu et al., 2014; Zhang et al., 2015b). Correspondingly, KXL tunnel had lower  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  EF due to its high proportion of gasoline vehicles (Cui et al., 2016).

metadata.title: Fig. 1. (a) Shenzhen is located on the southeast coast of China; (b) the sampling site of this study and its surrounding environment in the Pearl River Delta region (satellite images from Google Earth).
references: As presented in Fig. 1, Shenzhen is located in the PRD of Guangdong Province, China. The sampling site for this study was located on the campus of the Shenzhen Graduate School of Peking University (PKUSZ,  $22.60^{\circ}\mathrm{N}$ ,  $113.97^{\circ}\mathrm{E}$ ) in western Shenzhen, which is predominantly surrounded by green areas, teaching buildings and apartments. No evident anthropogenic emission sources of air pollutants surrounded the sampling site, except a highway approximately  $100\mathrm{m}$  away. Previous studies have shown that the concentrations of air pollutants such as  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and VOCs at this site were approximately equal to their average concentrations in the entire PRD region (Louie et al., 2013; Huang et al., 2018). Therefore, this site was considered suitable for long-term observation of trends in air pollutants in the PRD region. Table S1 summarizes the effective days of VOCs observed in each month from 2014 to 2019, with 1169 days in total over the six years of sampling. During the six-year sampling period, the loss of data was mainly due to instrument failure and bad weather, especially in the wet season due to frequent rainfall, resulting in a higher rate of missing data during

Shenzhen is located in China's Pearl River Delta (PRD) urban agglomeration, a region where rapid urbanization and industrialization have led to serious, complex atmospheric pollution (Sin et al., 2001; He et al., 2011). With the implementation of strict environmental protection measures in recent years, the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentration in Shenzhen has decreased significantly. From 2013 to 2019, the average value of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  in Shenzhen was reduced from 40 to  $24~\mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^3$  (Ecological Environment Bureau of Shenzhen Municipality, 2019). However, due to the high temperatures and high solar radiation characteristics of this region, atmospheric photochemical reactions are active, and the concentration of  $\mathrm{O_3}$  was increasing at an average annual rate of 0.7 ppb from 2014 to 2018 (Wang et al., 2020), such that  $\mathrm{O_3}$  is replacing  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  as the primary atmospheric pollutant in the region (Huang et al., 2019). Several previous short-term studies have observed the diurnal and seasonal patterns of OVOCs and identified their sources in this region (Liu et al., 2008; Liu et al., 2012; Louie et al., 2013; Han et al., 2019; Huang et al., 2019; Zhu et al., 2019); however, this is the first long-term study of OVOCs that permits the observation of interannual variability and long-term changes, in particular those related to emission reduction measures.

metadata.source_md: Does energy transition improve air quality_ Eviden.md
metadata.save_path: C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\Does energy transition improve air quality_ Eviden\figure_2.jpg
metadata.title: Fig. 2. Heating area and coal consumption (1997-2018).
references: Due to the lower temperatures in Northern China, burning coal for heating had become the most common way in which residents kept warm in winter. Since the 1950s, the Government of China provided winter heating via coal-fired boilers in the north Huai River areas. Northern cities received free unlimited heating from November 15 to March 15. Up until now, heating has not only been an important indicator of social wealth but has also become necessary for the survival of northern people in winter. According to statistical data released by the Ministry of Housing and Urban-Rural Development (MOHURD) of the People's Republic of China, the annual growth rate of the urban centralized heating area reached  $14.04\%$  during 1991-2017 (Fig. 2). At the same time, coal consumption increased under the rising demand for heating in the two decades prior to 2013. The "coal-to-gas/electricity" policy has generally altered the previous energy consumption structure. However, the rural heating energy consumption structure has largely remained unbalanced, among which coal consumption accounts for approximately  $75\%$ . Moreover, the large scattered coal heating demand has caused a series of environmental and social problems. The combustion of coal in boilers is associated with the release of air pollutants, especially  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  and total suspended particles (TSP). Winter heating has also reduced people's life expectancy in northern regions of China [27]. To meet the increasing demand for heating as well as to alleviate air pollutant emissions, the National Development and Reform Commission of the People's Republic of China announced its plan for the WCHP project (covering 2017-2021).

implementing intervention measures have seen substantial air quality improvements [7,8]. For example, the United States of America has replaced coal with natural gas in generating electric power since 1997 and has achieved  $44\%$  and  $40\%$  reduction in  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  and  $\mathrm{NO}_{\mathrm{x}}$  emissions from the 1997 levels [9]. A cleaner heating technologies program in New Zealand has led to the significant decline of PM emissions and concentrations [10]. Such policies aiming to diversify energy structures with clean and renewable energy have been successful in certain countries, which also has important implications for China [11]. In Northern China, the annual consumption of coal used for heating is approximately 400 million metric tons, nearly  $50\%$  of which is separately distributed for use in rural areas. Moreover, the combustion of scattered coal heating has caused severe haze to occur in winter [12]. Chen et al. found that the combustion of scattered coal heating has led to an approximately two-thirds increase in air pollutants during periods when heating is employed (i.e., winter) compared to periods when heating is not employed (i.e., summer) [13]. Compared to coal, clean energies such as natural gas and wind power emit less  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_{\mathrm{x}}$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ , and  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ , and thus reduce human health risks from exposure to air pollutants [9]. Therefore, switching from scattered coal

Studies on the effectiveness of energy transition have not produced a consensus among scholars. Lin and Zhu [18] found that fuel switching in "energy saving and emission reduction" (ESER) policy had failed to decrease  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations. Li and Chen [19] reported that a city that underwent coal-to-gas transition had no significant impact on reducing  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions, and this was primarily because the city focused on the replacement of industrial coal while ignoring the combustion of scattered coal heating in rural areas. Additionally, although Zhao and Luo [20] reported that a "coal-to-gas" project decreased pollutant emissions from a household sector by substituting civil urban coal consumption, they confirmed that this project had a limited effect on reducing industrial coal consumption. Liu et al. [21] adopted the GAINS model to examine the effect of coal switching strategy in Jing-Jin-Ji region, and estimated that the primary  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  decreased by  $28\%$  and  $11\%$ , respectively. Wu et al. [22] evaluated the household coal-replacement program in North China and concluded that coal-to-electricity policy achieved the goal of a clean winter, while the high-quality coal replacement policy had no improvement in indoor air quality. Since the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  (smog) crisis in the winter of 2013, environmental governance in China has shifted attention to scattered coal heating in rural areas, after which a series of policies were implemented and subsequently achieved remarkable results [23]. However, official reports may have overestimated the benefits of the clean heating policy by simply attributing the decrease in air pollutant concentrations to the promotion of clean heating policies while ignoring the interference of other exogenous energy-saving and emission-reduction policies [24]. Additionally, policy impacts will vary in different regions; thus, it is necessary to identify the validity of such policy among regions. Few studies to date have focused on the evaluation of energy transition policies in China. As an important part of China's energy transition process, it must be determined that whether or not the "Winter Clean Heating Pilot" (WCHP) project improves air quality. Answering this question can offer some guidance in China's future environmental governance while also providing a quantitative reference for other coal-based, energy-consuming developing countries. It could also be beneficial to achieve the synergy between emission reduction and air quality improvement under the background of climate change.

On the one hand, even though the WCHP project has proven to be an effective mean to control winter-derived heating emissions, the cost of this project should not be ignored. The conversion cost of "coal-to-gas/electricity" is higher and includes a subsequent increase in the cost of gas. Replacing unprocessed coal with clean coal can be used as a transitional method in the ultimate goal of energy transition for regions that experience considerable financial governmental pressure and low residential income. The government should focus on reforming the cost of electricity/gas by updating to more advanced heating technology and devices. On the other hand, since the reduction effect of the WCHP project on regional air quality varies under different development levels and resource distributions, project control should be delegated to regional governments based on local features rather than following the outdated pattern of "one-size-fits-all". In order to minimize the negative impact of clean heating policies on energy poverty and energy consumption inequality, the government should provide subsidies to clean energy more affordable to low-income groups.

metadata.title: Fig. 7. The variation trend of AQI in four seasons.
references: To conduct a thorough analysis of AQI variations based on seasonal patterns, the average AQI change rate in four seasons was computed, as shown in Fig. 7. The four seasons were divided based on meteorology, where spring ranges from March to May, summer from June to August, autumn from September to November, and winter from December to February of the next year. Results showed that the most significant decrease in AQI occurred in winter, with average rates of  $35.46\%$ ,  $30.23\%$ ,  $29.22\%$ ,  $25.90\%$ ,  $24.25\%$ ,  $22.37\%$  and  $6.63\%$  for the northeast, southern, central, eastern, northern, southwest, and northwest China, respectively. The AQI decrease in autumn was also substantial, and northeast China had the greatest decline, averaging  $35.77\%$  after the implementation of CCTPs. The AQI change rate in summer and spring was less than that of the other two seasons, with an average of  $15.81\%$  and  $13.41\%$ , respectively.
Fig. 6. The variation trend of AQI in the Chinese mainland.

metadata.title: Fig. 4. Summary of the major control regulations in China since 2013. PP: power plants; IN: industry; TR: transport; DO: domestic sector; AR: agriculture.
references: Fig. 4 summarizes the major action plans for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  control implemented by the Chinese government since 2013. The policy framework shows a clear trend of gradually involving more categories of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  precursors and emission sectors, since traditional control actions for a single pollutant or a single sector were insufficient to deliver substantial reductions in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations as required by the Action Plan. One notable case is that the estimated  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions and sulfate concentrations inversed from satellite observations both rebounded slightly during 2010-2011 due to a rapid increase in industrial activities and less stringent controls on the iron and steel industries (Fig. 2), which surpassed the reduction benefits of  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  controls on power plants [31]. As a result, a series of stringent control measures have been implemented in the industrial polluting sector, including strengthening industrial emissions standards, phasing out small-scale high-polluting factories, phasing out outdated industrial capacities, and upgrading industrial boilers [32]. Compared with the control measures for

As Fig. 4 indicates, control actions have been implemented nationwide, with additional efforts in several key regions. Improving air quality and mitigating  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution in core megacities (e.g., Beijing and Shanghai) have been attained due to increased attention from the central government and the public. Beijing provides a success story in tackling  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution after two-decade-long efforts. In fact, the implementation of comprehensive air pollution controls in Beijing was initiated as early as the 1990s. During the 2008 Olympic Games, extensive control measures were implemented in Beijing and surrounding provinces to guarantee good air quality [41]. Ambient concentrations of CO,  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ , and  $\mathrm{NO}_2$  continuously decreased, even as the municipal GDP, resident population, vehicle stock, and energy consumption increased significantly from 1998 to 2013. Owing to the success of air pollution controls for the 2008 Olympic Games, the local leaders recognized that ambient  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations resulted from a combination of emissions, meteorological conditions, and

From the perspective of the utilization characteristics of motor vehicles in Wuhan, the 10 selected motor vehicles include small domestic passenger cars, taxis, large and medium passenger buses, large and medium trucks and so on, which covered most kinds of the vehicles in Wuhan, China. The basic information of the 10 typical vehicles were shown in Table 1.

The ratio of OC/EC was an important parameter in the carbonaceous components of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ , which was often used to determine the source of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ . The OC/EC ratios of carbonaceous compositions from the 10 tested vehicles were shown in Fig. 4, and the average OC/EC ratios of the vehicles with different emission standards were figured out as well. The OC/EC ratio of gasoline vehicles was higher than 3.0 except for Vehicle 1#. It meant there were many organic components in the carbonaceous component, which was consistent with the results of some recent studies (James et al., 2002). The OC/EC ratio of Vehicle 1# was only 0.3, which deviated from the normal value of gasoline vehicle emissions. Because Vehicle 1# was a taxi with both gasoline and natural gas functions, and the engine used a mixture of gasoline and natural gas for a long time. It used gasoline fuel during the test, and insufficient combustion of gasoline in the engine caused an increase in the number of black carbon particles, which led to a small ratio of OC/EC. The average OC/EC ratio of China IV Vehicles was 10.2, and it decreased to 5.3 in China V gasoline vehicles. This indicated the improvement of emission standards could reduce the percentage of OC in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  emitted from gasoline vehicles. For diesel vehicles, the average OC/EC ratios of China III, IV and V vehicles were 1.5, 3.0, and 4.9, respectively. The OC/EC ratio of diesel vehicles turned to decrease as the emission standard rose up. It could also be seen from Fig. 4 that the OC/EC ratio of gasoline vehicles was generally greater than that of diesel vehicles, which was mainly because diesel was more likely to form unburned soot particles during the combustion process (Shen et al., 2021). There were two main differences between gasoline vehicles and diesel vehicles, fuel composition and engine structure. Gasoline was made of small molecules and was easy to mix evenly with air. metadata.title: Fig. 1. Trends in GDP, energy consumption, and population, as well as emissions of  SO_2 ,  NO_x , primary  PM_{2.5} , non-methane volatile organic compounds (NMVOCs), and  CO_2  between 1990 and 2017, with values for 1990 taken as 1. GDP, energy consumption, and population data were obtained from the National Bureau of Statistics of China. Emission data of air pollutants ( SO_2 ,  NO_x ,  PM_{2.5} , and NMVOCs) for 2010-2017 was obtained from Tsinghua University's multi-resolution emission inventory for China (MEIC) [5]. references: (Fig. 1 [5,6]). In 2017, approximately  $70\%$  of all 338 municipal cities in China still did not meet the National Ambient Air Quality Standard (NAAQS) [7], with non-attainment of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  as the most prominent air pollution problem. China faces the dual pressures of the improvement of national air quality and the mitigation of climate change.

On the other hand, regulations on  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  emissions in China have lagged in comparison with actions on  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ . The sources for  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  are distributed across multiple sectors involving high-temperature combustions, resulting in increased difficulty and higher costs for  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  emission mitigation. In addition to coal combustion processes, including thermal power and industrial plants, on-road vehicles and other fossil fuel combustion processes are important sources of  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  emissions. Total  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  emissions increased threefold during 1990-2010 (Fig. 1), as indicated by both bottom-up estimation and satellite observations [24]. Many large cities have experienced rapid motorization since 2000, resulting in their urban nitrogen dioxide  $(\mathrm{NO}_2)$  concentrations exceeding NAAQS. Increasing trends in the ratios of nitrate  $(\mathrm{NO}_3^-)$  to sulfate  $(\mathrm{SO}_4^{2-})$  in both ambient  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  mass [25] and precipitation [10] have been observed in many regions of China. However, national mandates on controlling  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  emissions were not established until the 12th FYP (2011-2015).

metadata.source_md: Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven.md
metadata.save_path: C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven\figure_5.jpg
metadata.title: Fig. 5. AQI regional spatial autocorrelation in 31 provincial cities of the Chinese mainland.
references: After the spatial gatherings of air pollution are confirmed, to better identify the gathering district, a regional spatial autocorrelation analysis is also conducted. As shown in Fig. 5, there were still high-high gathering areas (high levels of pollution) and low-low gathering areas (low levels of pollution) from 2013 to 2020. Meanwhile, a north-south spatial heterogeneity pattern was obvious, depicting the high levels of pollution in northern China and low levels of pollution in southern China.

Clean and efficient use of coal in China entered the implementation stage in 2015, and a series of policies were established to promote the sustainable development of clean coal technologies (CCTs). However, the long-term effects of the clean coal technology policies (CCTPs) have not been evaluated, and the outbreak of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) would bring great uncertainty regarding the effectiveness evaluation. Employing deep learning approaches and spatial autocorrelation analysis, the present work intended to explore the air quality variation before and after the implementation of CCTPs, and investigate the association between air quality and socioeconomic factors to explore the internal mechanism of air quality improvement. Results showed that after implementing CCTPs, the air quality index (AQI) had an average reduction of  $18.82\%$  and the identical drop in air pollution in 2018 implied a 2-year time lag of CCTPs. Additionally, emission reduction and deindustrialization were explored as two promising ways to improve air quality while promoting energy transformation. The findings indicated that the Chinese government ought to pay more attention to long-term plans; industrial restructuring and environmental protection should be insisted upon to realize sustainable energy development. By providing a perspective on air quality improvement after the policies are implemented, this study can lead practitioners and academia to a comprehensive and objective view of clean coal policies.

To accelerate energy transformation and reduce air pollution, both central and provincial governments in China are taking increasingly leading roles in shaping policy outcomes, through adopting cleaner production or energy saving policies, and revisions of local standards or emission reduction. Since the Chinese energy structure is characterized by being rich in coal, poor in oil, and lack of gas, thus a series of coal-togas (CTG) and coal-to-electricity (CTE) policies are established to promote the clean conversion of coal resources. Further, coal-fired stoves were the most popular source of heating in winter in Northern China, which are cheap and easily available, however, would lead to heavy emissions of various air contaminants (Fan et al., 2020). Strategies to control residential coal combustion (RCC) have been a focus since 2016. Accordingly, there is growing attention from researchers to examine the effectiveness of RCC, CTG, and CTE policies (Xue et al., 2016; Chen and Chen, 2019; Chen et al., 2022). Applying a comprehensive perspective of economic, energy, and environmental impact, Lin and Jia (2020) researched that the CTE policy is an effective pattern to eliminate  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  and  $\mathrm{NO}_{\mathrm{x}}$  emissions, more than half of residents' emissions could be cut down, and the co-benefit between CTE and other energy saving policies was observed. As for the implementation of the RCC policy, Li et al. (2018) estimated that air quality in Beijing would be remarkably improved if residential coal in the BTH region and the surrounding areas are replaced by other clean energy sources. Similar results have been obtained in Xue et al.'s research (2016) as well, using scenario analysis, the projected emissions of primary pollutants from RCC showed that it is difficult to eliminate the emissions from residential sources only rely on mandatory control measures, adopting multiple kinds of policy instruments to promote the clean conversion of coal resources is a preferable pathway (Xue et al., 2016). Yu et al. (2021) examined the dynamic effects of CTG policy and its impact mechanism on air pollution, revealing that this policy has significantly improved air quality in 274 Chinese prefecture-level cities, with a reduction of  $31.3\%$  for industrial  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $36\%$  for industrial smoke, and  $33.1\%$  for AQI.

The Chinese mainland is composed of 33 administrative regions, however, the policies implemented by Hong Kong and Macao special administrative regions differ from the other provinces. Moreover, coal-dominated factories and enterprises are mainly located in the capital city of these provinces, which are typical epitomes of regional environmental pollution. Therefore, a total of 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland are selected. The system architecture of this study is depicted in Fig. 2.

To further analyze the temporal and spatial variation features of air quality in 31 provincial capital cities from 2013 to 2020, the spatial autocorrelation analysis method is applied. ArcGIS 10.6 and GeoDa software is used for the calculation and analytic process.

The study obtained the data including a total of 1440 daily air quality and meteorological data (January 22, 2016, to December 31, 2019) from 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland,  $60\%$  of which are chosen as the training set,  $20\%$  as the validation set and  $20\%$  as the test set. After determining the best basic network architecture for the current prediction tasks, models are set up in units of cities, and the training set is used to train the tailored CNN-LSTM models. The accuracy indicators of the model prediction results of 31 cities, namely RMSE values averaging 22.06, models perform well overall, indicating that the applied CNN-LSTM network has good capabilities for AQI prediction (Jin et al., 2020; Mao et al., 2021).

Global spatial autocorrelation analysis is carried out using the average monthly AQIs for the 31 studied cities from 2013 to 2020. As shown in Table 3, the results of global Moran's I are all greater than 0 and statistically significant, indicating that there are significant and positive spatial correlations among the average monthly AQIs. In summary, there is an obvious regional integration tendency of air pollution in Chinese cities.
metadata.concise_summary: The figure displays annual spatial autocorrelation of AQI in 31 Chinese provincial cities from 2013 to 2020, highlighting high-high and low-low pollution areas.
metadata.inferred_insight: The figure illustrates regional AQI patterns, showing reduced pollution in northern China post-policy implementation, supporting the effectiveness of air quality improvements.

metadata.source_md: 一种工业热源提取方法_利用温度特征模板的BP神经网络.md
metadata.save_path: C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\一种工业热源提取方法_利用温度特征模板的BP神经网络\figure_4.jpg
metadata.title: Fig.4 Number of industrial heat source objects and industrial heat anomaly points in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas
metadata.supplement: (a) 工业热源对象 (b) 工业热源热异常点 图4 京津冀及周边地区工业热源对象和工业热异常点数量
references: Abstract: Aiming at the problem of insufficient quantity and spatial refinement in the extraction of industrial heat source from annual scale thermal anomaly data, a neural network industrial heat source extraction method based on temperature feature template is proposed by using VIIRS active fire data. This study took Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas as the study area, Firstly, according to the spatial aggregation characteristics of industrial heat sources, the heat source objects were divided by the OPTICS algorithm. Secondly, according to the thermal radiation characteristics of the heat sources, the temperature characteristic template of industrial heat sources and non- industrial heat sources were constructed. Finally, the BP neural network was used to extract industrial heat source objects using the temperature feature template and heat source statistical characteristics as parameters. The results show that: (1) the extraction precision of industrial heat source of the neural network algorithm of temperature feature template proposed in this paper reached  $96.31\%$ . Compared with time filtering and logistic regression methods, the extraction precision of industrial heat sources was improved by  $8.45\%$  and  $7.53\%$ , respectively; (2) From 2015 to 2020, the number of industrial heat sources in the six provinces and cities in Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas decreased by  $27.46\%$ . The number of industrial heat source objects and heat anomalies in Hebei Province decreased by  $8.06\%$  and  $7.44\%$  annually, respectively, which was the largest decrease compared with other provinces and cities. The

concentration of industrial heat sources in Shandong and Tianjin increased by  $25.72\%$  and  $86.64\%$ , respectively, indicating that the industrial transformation and upgrade policies in the two places have achieved remarkable results; (3) Tangshan, Handan, Lvliang, and Changzhi accounted for  $31.37\%$  of the total industrial heat sources in the study area, which are the main cities in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas. The degree of industrial heat source accumulation and energy consumption in seven cities such as Linfen and Taiyuan was higher than those in other cities; The degree of industrial heat source accumulation and energy consumption in 11 cities such as Beijing and Zhoukou was lower than those in other cities; (4) From January to May 2020, the number of industrial heat anomalies in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas remained unchanged or increased compared with the same period in 2019 and 2021. The COVID-19 had no significant impact on the industrial heat source in the study area. The number of industrial heat anomalies in Wuhan in January and February 2020 decreased by more than  $66.67\%$  compared with that in the same period in 2019 and 2021, the number of industrial heat anomalies from March to May 2020 was lower than that in the same period of 2019. The COVID-19 has had a significant impact on industrial heat sources in Wuhan from January to May 2020. This study reflects the current situation and trend of industrial heat source development in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas, which provides a valuable reference for the formulation and adjustment of relevant policies such as reducing energy consumption and improving secondary industry concentration.
Key words: industrial heat source; OPTICS algorithm; temperature feature template; neural network; VIIRS Active Fire; Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas

(d) Slope_NFHWH

content.title: Fig. 3. The analysis framework of the sources for energy increase in the passenger transport sector in China.
content.summary: The diagram outlines the sources of energy increase in China's passenger transport sector, detailing sub-sectors and influential factors like population and vehicle stock structure.
content.insight: The framework highlights the impact of policy measures on energy efficiency, emphasizing transportation structure adjustment and fuel economy improvements for significant energy savings.

metadata.source_md: Energy growth sources and future energy-saving pot.md

metadata.title: Fig. 3. The analysis framework of the sources for energy increase in the passenger transport sector in China.
references: analysis framework is proposed to consider different characteristics of traffic modes, as shown in Fig. 3. The effects of main driving factors on total passenger transport energy use are analyzed briefly. Furthermore, a specific investigation into three subsectors of passenger transport is performed in turn.
# Energy growth sources and future energy-saving potentials in passenger transportation sector in China

In China, energy use from passenger vehicles is increasing rapidly due to urbanization and income growth, but the contributions of transport service structure shift and energy efficiency improvement, especially future energy-saving directions are controversial and unclear because of the differences in the choices of traffic modes, which may greatly affect the analysis results and their interpretations. This study investigates the growth sources of energy use in the passenger transport sector during 1979-2017 and future energy-saving potentials by 2030 by re-examining statistical data. Results show that the transport service increase and transport structure shift led to the increase in energy use by  $665.25\%$  and  $337.22\%$ , but approximately  $14\%$  of the increased energy use was offset by energy efficiency improvements, and its effect is limited in public transportation sector, compared with private passenger vehicle sector. In public passenger transport sector, adjusting the service or volume structure may be a desirable option while in private passenger vehicle sector the policy focus is more on improving fuel efficiency and reducing average annual kilometers traveled per capita. Overall, there is still at least  $28.3\%$  of energy reduction potential by energy efficiency improvement before 2030 compared to 2017 baseline level. Potential evaluation showed that the current potential of transport service structure adjustment based on industrial planning is limited, which highlights the necessity of reform, involving promoting the sharing transportation, railway, subway and buses.

In the last two decades, the transport sector has become a major source of energy consumption worldwide. In China, transport energy use increased from 58.63 Mtce (million tons of coal equivalent) in 1995 to 421.91 Mtce in 2017, with an annual average growth rate of nearly  $10\%$ . Energy use from passenger transport dominates total transport energy use, accounting for nearly  $60\%$  in 2017. By 2030, more than 300 million new rural residents are expected to enter the cities in China, which will have a great impact on the passenger transport energy consumption. Passengers are relatively easily guided by policy measures to change their energy-using behaviors in their daily travel, thus leading to low-carbon

However, one of the key challenges is that the current energy statistics of passenger transportation are fragmented in China. The lack of comprehensive statistic specification causes that the data is scattered into different sectors in energy statistical system. The data does not properly cover metrics of interest for energy consumption analysis of passenger transportation. Part of the data is covered in the Transport, Storage and Post sector and Residential Consumption sector in China Energy Statistical Yearbook [3]. The former only includes the intercity public passenger transport and

Many studies have investigated energy consumption and emissions increase in transport sector [2,5,6]. The relationship between transport energy use and socio-economic factors has also been intensively discussed using econometric models [7-11]. Various options to save energy and reduce emissions in the transport sector have been evaluated for their reduction potential, from both the technological and behavioral perspectives [1,12,13]. In transport studies, bottom-up methodology and scenario analysis were often used to study future trends, mitigation options, and reduction potential [14,15]. In particular, road transport and passenger transport have always been the hot topics for evaluating energy consumption and emissions [16,17].

Either from an economy-wide or a sectoral perspective, the driving forces of increased energy consumption have been extensively investigated [18-20]. In the transportation sector, these studies could be summarized from two perspectives, namely, 1) the sectoral perspective, and 2) the vehicle-based perspective. From the sectoral perspective, changes in energy consumption and emission have been analyzed either for the whole transportation sector or its subsectors. Activity factors, namely Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth, population increase, and propensity to travel, were often found to be the main drivers of growing energy consumption [21-24], which is similar to the passenger transport subsector [25-27]. Other studies have focused more on micro sections of transportation. For example, on the vehicle level, the vehicle population, fuel mix, and vehicle fuel efficiency were

With accelerating urbanization in China, passenger transport energy use has rapidly increased, from 28.78 Mtce in 1997 to 252.47 Mtce in 2017, with an annual average growth rate of  $11.47\%$  (Fig. 1). Energy use could thus be divided into three development stages. From 1978 to 1990, the intercity public passenger transport was the main traveling mode of passengers, as the urban and the private vehicle subsectors were undeveloped. From 1990 to 2002, energy consumption of the private passenger vehicles gradually caught up with that of the intercity subsector. From 2003 to 2017, the private subsector became the major source of the passenger transport energy use. The energy intensity of passenger transport decreased continually due to technological advances, and the energy intensity of the private passenger vehicles decreased rapidly from 143.68 Tce/Mp-km (ton coal equivalent/million passenger-kilometers) in 1997 to 51.38 Tce/Mp-km in 2017, due to the policy of fuel consumption limits for passenger cars [48]. This corresponds to a huge reduction in fuel consumption. Without this reduction in energy intensity, the fuel consumption in the private passenger transport sector would have increased nearly three times greater than it is today.

(c) Modal energy intensity  
Fig. 7. Energy consumption and passenger volume share of three modes of the urban public passenger transport, 1978-2017. Data sources: China Statistical Yearbook [4]; China Urban Construction Statistical Yearbook [69]; Statistics bulletin of transportation sector development [64]; Fuel consumption limits for passenger cars, Fuel consumption evaluation methods and targets for passenger cars [48] and the authors' personal calculation.

The data includes socio-economic activity, transport structure and energy intensity. The socio-economic activity data includes the population, the number of households and the GDP, which are from the China Statistical Yearbook [4]; the transport-related activity data includes the passenger service of the intercity public passenger transport, the passenger volume of the urban public passenger transport and the vehicle population of the private passenger vehicles, which are from 1) Passenger Kilometers of the Transport, Postal and Telecommunication Services, and Software Industry, 2) Basic Statistics on Public Transportation in Cities by Region of the Urban, Rural and Regional Development, 3) Possession of Private Vehicles of the Transport, Postal and Telecommunication Services, and Software Industry in the China Statistical Yearbook [4]. The structure data consists of the service structure of the intercity public modes, the volume structure of the urban public modes and the vehicle type structure of the private subsector, which are calculated based on the above transport-related activity data. The intensity data includes the energy intensity of the three subsectors and the average annual mileage of the private passenger vehicles, which are from the Yearbook of China Integrated transport [63], the Statistics bulletin of transportation sector development [64], the Railway Statistics [65], and the Fuel consumption limits for passenger cars [48]. The average annual mileage of the private passenger vehicles is from the survey data in the FEEI model [49].

The GDP per capita effect was the dominant contributor to energy consumption growth and its contribution increased over time, but the contribution decreased during 1996-2000 (Figs. 5b) and 2008-2010 (Fig. 5c) due to the Asian financial crisis and the global financial crisis respectively. The shift of various modes of passenger transport (structure effect) contributed to the energy consumption decrease in the years leading up to 1990 because the passenger service share of civil aviation decreased (Fig. 5c). Since 1990, the structure effect turned from a negative value to a positive value. After 1990, the highway and civil aviation services were promoted vigorously by the State Council and Civil Aviation Administration of China [50,66]. Therefore, adjusting the transport structure in the intercity subsector, such as promoting the transfer form highway and civil aviation to railway, could be an effective way to save energy. The passenger service intensity effect provides an increasingly negative contribution to the increase in energy consumption during 1978-2017. The energy intensity of the railway, waterway, and civil aviation services decreased rapidly due to technological advancements and improved management.

The GDP per capita effect was the dominant contributor to energy consumption growth due to rapid economic development in China. The most prominent contribution appeared during 1991-1995 and 2006-2010, with an energy consumption increase of  $275\%$  and  $195\%$ , respectively (Fig. 6b). The passenger volume structure effect was another main driver of the energy consumption growth due to the shift in the dominant mode of transport. From the late 1980s to the 1990s, the taxi industry grew rapidly, owing to the policy of open business implemented by the State Council

The multiplication decomposition results in the three subsectors are shown in Fig. 9. Five influential factors on energy consumption can be observed and are quite similar in their effects in the intercity and urban passenger transport subsectors. The GDP per capita increase was always the dominant contributor to rises in energy consumption, but this effect was larger in the urban subsector (2.713). The passenger volume/service per GDP played a critical role in reducing energy consumption as opposed to energy intensity in the public transportation sector. In contrast, however, the private transport subsector showed that fuel efficiency improvement, decline of family size and average annual kilometers per capita offset the effect of vehicle stock growth per capita together.

This study contributes to understanding the underlying driving factors of energy growth and exploring energy efficiency improvement opportunities in passenger transport sector in China. First, a comprehensive picture of passenger transport energy consumption trends during 1979-2017 in China is given. Main drivers of energy growth are briefly identified through re-classifying the fragmented data statistics, which, to our knowledge, are unique and original. Second, the effects of the main driving forces of increased energy consumption are investigated firstly by using a hybrid decomposition method. Third, the future energy-saving potentials of different policy pathways until 2020 and 2030 are estimated and compared involving sharing transportation, structure adjustment, energy efficiency improvement, and urban car ownership control.

The results show that there are diversified contributors to energy consumption depending on the traffic modes utilized. On the whole, GDP growth per capita, motorization level and shifts in service structure mainly contribute to energy consumption increase in passenger transport sector during the period 1979-2017, and they cause more than 10 times increase in energy consumption, but the actual energy consumption increased just by 8.77 times because of the effect of energy efficiency improvement. For the intercity and urban public passenger transport, the GDP growth per capita, the service or volume structure change, and population growth strongly dominate the changes in energy consumption, and the effect of energy efficiency improvement is not very significant on a relative basis. In the private passenger vehicle sector, however, the situation is significantly different. Fuel efficiency improvement, decline of family size and average annual kilometers traveled per capita together offset the positive impacts from motorization level rise and vehicle type structure change. Especially, the fuel efficiency improvement has enormously contributed to energy conservation since 2010 in China.

Outlook analysis shows that by 2030 energy conservation potential in intercity public passenger transport sector is limited (16.6%), but there are still huge energy-saving potentials in urban public passenger transport sector (54.3%) and private passenger transport sector (34.3%). They could totally lead to 31.6% of energy consumption reduction in passenger transport sector by 2030.

In order to reduce energy use in passenger transport sector, multiple policy directions deserve attention. In China, the motorization level could be regulated by some mandatory policies, such as the vehicle license lottery in major cities, such as Beijing and Tianjin. In future, it is essential to give priority to public transport and sharing transport, such as the subway, bus, car-sharing, and bike-sharing. Particularly, first, sharing transportation deserves more attention in China because Chinese people are more likely to accept sharing and collective ideas, and there are less regulatory restrictions in China. Second, the energy intensity reduction targets up until 2020 need to be strictly enforced, especially for fuel efficiency standards for private passenger vehicles. Third, low-intensity transport modes, such as railway, waterway, rail transit, and mini vehicles, are strongly recommended to promote the low-carbon transition.

metadata.title: Fig. 4. Comparisons of annual emissions for China from different studies. The emissions are for year 2005 unless noted. The range line represents the  95\%  CIs around the central estimates of this study.
references: Figure 4 compares the central estimates of Chinese national emissions by this work with those of other studies. It can be seen that most estimates are within the  $95\%$  CIs calculated in the current study, except for  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_{\mathrm{x}}$  and BC emissions by Ohara et al. (2007). Interannual variability can partly explain the lower estimates of  $\mathrm{NO}_{\mathrm{x}}$  and BC by Ohara et al. (2007), which provided an emission inventory

Uncertainties introduced by energy statistics and applications of non-Chinese emission factors have been estimated in previous Chinese emission inventory studies. Streets et al. (2003) applied expert judgment, in which the coefficients of variation (i.e., standard deviation divided by the mean) of the activity levels were assumed based on measures of economic development and perceived statistical quality, and those of emission factors were based on the reliability ratings of U.S. emission factors (USEPA, 2002). They estimated that the uncertainties of Chinese emissions varied from  $-12\% \sim 13\%$  for  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  to  $-83\% \sim 495\%$  for organic carbon (expressed as the lower and upper bounds of a  $95\%$  confidence interval, CI, around a central estimate). To investigate discrepancies in the trends of bottom-up  $\mathrm{NO}_{\mathrm{x}}$  emissions and of the  $\mathrm{NO}_2$  column by satellite, Zhang et al. (2007a) set alternative emission scenarios in which energy statistics from different sources and emission factors with different control levels were applied. They concluded that  $\mathrm{NO}_{\mathrm{x}}$  emissions in east-central China in 2004 could range from 7.9 to  $9.3\mathrm{Tg}$ , and the annual growth rate of emissions from 1995 to 2004 could range from  $5.5\%$  to  $7.1\%$ . Other studies conducted preliminary uncertainty analysis of certain species or sectors in China using statistical methods. Bond et al. (2004) assumed that most emission factors of particulate matter (PM) followed lognormal distributions (with the exception of those of diesel vehicles, which had a gamma distribution) and evaluated the uncertainties of global black carbon (BC) and organic carbon (OC) emissions. The  $95\%$  CIs for Chinese emissions were estimated to be  $-37\% \sim 147\%$  and  $-44\% \sim 104\%$ , respectively. Wu et al. (2010) focused on

metadata.title: Fig. 2. The system architecture of this study.
references: The Chinese mainland is composed of 33 administrative regions, however, the policies implemented by Hong Kong and Macao special administrative regions differ from the other provinces. Moreover, coal-dominated factories and enterprises are mainly located in the capital city of these provinces, which are typical epitomes of regional environmental pollution. Therefore, a total of 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland are selected. The system architecture of this study is depicted in Fig. 2.

Considering the data features of high-dimension, multivariable, nonlinearity and temporal-spatial correlation, and with the application of Keras and TensorFlow, a predictive model combining CNN and LSTM is applied and tailored in this study. The architecture and workflows are shown in Fig. 4 and summarized as follows.

The study obtained the data including a total of 1440 daily air quality and meteorological data (January 22, 2016, to December 31, 2019) from 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland,  $60\%$  of which are chosen as the training set,  $20\%$  as the validation set and  $20\%$  as the test set. After determining the best basic network architecture for the current prediction tasks, models are set up in units of cities, and the training set is used to train the tailored CNN-LSTM models. The accuracy indicators of the model prediction results of 31 cities, namely RMSE values averaging 22.06, models perform well overall, indicating that the applied CNN-LSTM network has good capabilities for AQI prediction (Jin et al., 2020; Mao et al., 2021).

content.title: Fig. 3. Procedures, the corresponding methods and principles of data preprocessing.

metadata.source_md: Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven.md
metadata.save_path: C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven\figure_3.jpg
metadata.title: Fig. 3. Procedures, the corresponding methods and principles of data preprocessing.
references: Considering that 2013-2015 is set as the period before CCTPs implementation and that 2016-2020 is the period after the policy node, thus the air pollution condition in 2020 is forecasted using data from 2016 to 2019. After extraction, the daily air quality data and the daily meteorological data of 31 cities are combined to generate a tabular dataset and train the predictive network, ranging from January 22, 2016 to December 31, 2019. Data preprocessing is conducted to feed the subsequent neural networks with complete and low noise data, the four procedures are listed below and shown in Fig. 3.

First, AQI prediction is a regression problem of time-series data, and continuous data are required. Therefore, missing values in the original dataset are filled by Lagrange interpolation, which is conducted using low-order interpolation polynomials in sections (Yang and Sarkar, 2007; Wubetie, 2017). Second, some errors would generate in the process of data acquisition, transmission and storage owing to uncontrollable elements. To eliminate the negative impacts of the abnormal data, a box plot is created to find the outliers. Then, the identified outliers are replaced with null values and filled up through linear interpolation. By doing so, most of the abnormal data are modified, and the quality of the dataset for prediction is greatly improved. Third, a predictive model is established for multivariate prediction, the daily  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ ,  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{CO}$ , and  $\mathrm{O}_3$  concentrations and the daily meteorological data are all applied. Specifically, to promote the efficiency of multivariable prediction, the Kendall correlation coefficient is utilized to analyze the correlation between the AQI and the mentioned parameters for each studied city (Ghayekhloo et al., 2015), as the Kendall coefficient is the rank correlation coefficient, and has a good performance in handling ordinal categorical variables which do not obey the normal distribution, conforming the feature of this studied dataset. If the coefficient is less than 0.1, then the corresponding parameter column will be deleted during the prediction process. Finally, Z-score normalization is conducted for the multidimensional data columns to diminish the magnitude differences of the data and speed up the convergence rate of the predictive model. After data preprocessing, a random 6:2:2 split of the data is applied to the training, validation and test set, respectively. At this point, all the input data are ready to be used in the prediction process.
metadata.concise_summary: Figure 3 depicts the procedures of data preprocessing with steps for missing data imputation, outlier treatment, correlation analysis, and standardization.
metadata.inferred_insight: The figure illustrates a comprehensive data preprocessing pipeline essential for preparing accurate input data for predictive modeling in air quality forecasting.

metadata.title: Fig. 4. The architecture of the tailored CNN-LSTM network.
references: Considering the data features of high-dimension, multivariable, nonlinearity and temporal-spatial correlation, and with the application of Keras and TensorFlow, a predictive model combining CNN and LSTM is applied and tailored in this study. The architecture and workflows are shown in Fig. 4 and summarized as follows.

As shown in Fig. 4, the general architecture of the predictive model is an encoder-decoder structure. The first half of the network is CNN and is utilized for feature extraction of input data. The latter half is LSTM, analyzing the features extracted by CNN and then forecasting the AQI of the next point in time.

backpropagation and learning the input sequence with longer time steps. Further, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) have a remarkable advantage in extracting data features and reducing dimensions, which could alleviate the problems of slow training and overfitting in fully connected networks (Alzubaidi et al., 2021). Correspondingly, many variants of the CNN-LSTM combination have been constructed for capturing the spatiotemporal dependencies in air quality forecasting tasks (Donahue et al., 2015; Vinyals et al., 2015; Qin et al., 2019), as it would create a great synergy than the individual models on their own. Specifically, first performing feature extraction using CNN, and then inputting the feature values into the LSTM architecture.

The study obtained the data including a total of 1440 daily air quality and meteorological data (January 22, 2016, to December 31, 2019) from 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland,  $60\%$  of which are chosen as the training set,  $20\%$  as the validation set and  $20\%$  as the test set. After determining the best basic network architecture for the current prediction tasks, models are set up in units of cities, and the training set is used to train the tailored CNN-LSTM models. The accuracy indicators of the model prediction results of 31 cities, namely RMSE values averaging 22.06, models perform well overall, indicating that the applied CNN-LSTM network has good capabilities for AQI prediction (Jin et al., 2020; Mao et al., 2021).
